title: The Stars Align
description: Fast-forward a field of moving light points to the exact second they converge into a readable ASCII message.
writeup:
## Approach

Each point has a position and a constant velocity. At time `t`, point `i` is at `(x + vx·t, y + vy·t)`. The message appears when the points are most tightly packed — i.e. when the **bounding box is minimised**.

## Finding the right time

The bounding-box height is a convex function of `t` (it decreases, hits a minimum, then increases again). That makes it a perfect candidate for **ternary search**:

- Maintain a `[lo, hi]` range (0 to 20 000 seconds covers all real inputs).
- Each iteration, evaluate at the two trisection points `m1` and `m2` and discard the third with the larger height.
- When the range narrows to ≤ 2, brute-force the remaining candidates.

This finds the answer in O(log n) height evaluations instead of scanning every second.

## Rendering the message

Once `t` is known, project all points, shift so the minimum coordinate is at the origin, and place `#` at every occupied cell — the result is the ASCII art letter grid.

Part 1 returns the rendered grid; Part 2 returns `t`.

## Code Highlights

- Ternary search replaces a brute-force scan of 10 000+ seconds.
- `render()` builds the grid in a single set-lookup comprehension.
- Visualizer shows the dot-cloud converging from a broad scale down to the message frame, sampling sparse frames early on and dense frames near `t`.
